titleStmt.title: National Library of Sweden, A 201
titleStmt.sponsor: Medieval Latin Manuscripts in the National Library: Cataloguing and Digitization
titleStmt.funder: Riksbankens jubileumsfond – The Swedish Foundation for Humanities and Social Sciences

publicationStmt.publisher: manuscripta.se: A Digital Catalogue of Medieval and Early Modern Manuscripts in Sweden

supportDesc.foliation: Modern foliation in pencil 1-146. diuine laudis

collation.signatures: Quire signatures in black ink on the verso of the last leaf of quires 1-11, the last one on .
collation.catchwords: Catchwords on the last leaf of quires 1-11, 13-14, and 16-18.

supportDesc.condition: The paste down on is missing. Remnants of its text visible as an off-set print.

p.note: Top left corner: National Library book label with the current signum in black ink.

binding.p: Medieval binding. Brown leather over wooden boards. 3 double raised bands and 2 endbands. 2 hooked clasps attached to red leather straps. 2 catchplates on . On stamped in gold: A 201. On two paper labels with text in black ink (barely readable due to wear): Exempla sanctorum et miracula et primo de beata virgine maria Sermones breues de tempore Sermo de passione domini cum aliis. Exposicio dominice oracionis et alia multa; on the second label an old signum, I 14, or (F 14) corrected to I 15 which is written both on the label and on the cover.
binding.condition: The brown colour of the leather is worn off in places. Spine is cracked, now reinforced with linen fabric. The pastedown on is missing, leaving the board attachments visible. The leather on is cracked.

origin.p: Germany , 14th-century, late, to 15th century, early. Origin and dating based on the script, exact origin and date unknown.

provenance.p: Belonged to the Erfurt Charterhouse as evidenced by the addition in the upper margin on : Cartusie Erfordiensis.

acquisition.p: Purchased from George Stephens 23 October 1848 as evidenced by the addition in the lower right corner on : Inköpt till Kgl. Bibl. från G. Stephens 23/10 1848.

msItem.title: Sermones de tempore
msItem.incipit: Hora est iam nos de sompno surgere Ad Ro xiii In hac epistula invitat nos apostolus ad quatuor Primo monet de sompno surgere
msItem.explicit: primus panis carnalis uel corporalis secundus racionalis tercius spiritualis et cetera

msItem.title: Martyrdoms of saints
msItem.incipit: Nota quod mortes sanctorum et tribulaciones vindicantur Mors domini uindicatur a tyto et vespasiano
msItem.explicit: Katherina habet in multis tribulacionibus subuenire et cetera.
msItem.finalRubric: Expliciunt auctoritates de sanctis per manus
msItem.note: Martyrdoms of the following saints: Andrew, Nicholas, Lucy, Thomas (apostle), John, Thomas Becket, Silvester, Paul of Thebes, Gregory, Benedict, Patrick, George, Adalbert, Mark, Philip (apostle), James the Minor, Pancras, Gervasius and Protasius, John the Baptist, John and Paul, Peter (apostle), Paul (apostle), Margaret, Mary Magdalene, Martha, Cyriacus, Lawrence, Bartholomew, Giles, Matthew, Maurice, Cosmas and Damian, Wenceslaus, Stanislaus, Jerome, Denis, Leonard, ten thousand martyrs, Simon and Jude, Martin, Elizabeth, Cecilia, Clemens, and Catherine.

msItem.title: On sins
msItem.incipit: Gregorius dicit missa dum cantetur peccata tam viuorum tam mortuorum condonantur
msItem.explicit: quia missa inuita audita ualet ad dimissionem culpe ad remissionem pene et ad augmentum gracie et cetera
msItem.note: Also in Köpenhamn, Royal Danish Library, Gl. kgl. S. 1360 4o, f. 24v.

msItem.title: A brief text on sins and a list of the ten commandments
msItem.note: These two texts are probably additions to an otherwise blank page. The first text begins: Nota peccata clamancia Sunt tria que numquam cessant clamare. The second begins: Nota decem precepta ydola sperne dei nomen non.

msItem.title: On the passion of Christ
msItem.explicit: ab eo duplici stola inmortalitatis videlicet corporis et anime decorari et regnum eternum cum ipso possidere Quod nobis prestare dignetur ipse dominus noster Ihesus christus qui cum patre et sancto spiritu viuit et regnat deus per omnia secula seculorum Amen
msItem.finalRubric: Explicit sermo de passione totali domini nostri ihesu christi
msItem.note: The beginning of the text is missing on account of at least one missing leaf. It probably contained a prologue since the first extant rubric, on reads: de prima parte. The second part begins on , the third part on and the fourth part on . Parts 2-4 account for each hour of Christ's passion with the hours added in red in the outer margin.

msItem.title: On sins
msItem.incipit: Sanctus bernardus dicit quot octo sunt que a peccatis nos reuocant et sunt de essencia hominis que si homo diligenter consideraret in eternum non peccaret
msItem.explicit: temptacionum feruor utriusque hominis statum affixerit Eligamus pocius ardere quam cedere
msItem.note:
The beginning of the text is a paraphrase of a passage in Jacobus de Voragine's Legenda aurea
, De circumcisione domini. The end of the text is a citation from Bernard of Clairvaux, In vigilia nativitatis domini, Sermo III, PL
.

msItem.title: On the passion of Christ
msItem.incipit: Qualiter per memoriam passionis domini nostri ihesu christi repellitur temptacio Nota bene
msItem.explicit: id est in speculacione videbis quod nobis prestare dignetur pater et filius et spiritus sanctus Cottidie igitur predicta in corde tuo permeditare
msItem.note: In the margin is added in black ink by a contemporary hand: Memoria passionis christi.

msItem.title: Collection of excerpts
msItem.incipit: Hec sunt quindecim signa ante diem Iudicii que erunt In quindecim diebus que beatus Ieronimus invenit in libris ebreorum
msItem.explicit: Item securis siue ferrum supernatans aquam et sic est finis et cetera
msItem.note: This section contains 44 notabilia treating for instance the dignity of priests, confession, Mass, the conversion of sinners, the seven deadly sins, the gospels. The first text is the list of the fifteen signs before Judgement and 15 signa has been added in the outer margin by a contemporary hand. The last text is on the efficacy of prayer, with the heading De oracione notabile bonum.

msItem.title: On the twelve degrees of humility (Excerpts from the Rule of St Benedict)
msItem.incipit: Clamat nobis scriptura diuina fratres dicens omnis qui se exaltat humiliabitur (Luke 8:14)
msItem.explicit: Ideo angustam viam arripuit et cetera
msItem.note:
Listed in Bloomfield et al. (1979)
. The first section, , is Ch. 7 of The Rule of St Benedict . The last section, , is an excerpt from the beginning of Ch. 5. In the top margin on is added in red ink by a contemporary hand: De gradibus humilitatis.

msItem.title: Two excerpts from Jerome and Gregory
msItem.incipit: Beatus Ieronimus Monachus viuat in monasterio sub vnius disciplina patris
msItem.explicit: Vna quippe et summa est probacio karitatis si et ipse diligitur qui aduersatur
msItem.note:
The first text is taken from Pseudo-Jerome Homilia ad monachos, PL 30
. The second consists of passages excerpted from Gregory I, Homilies on the Gospels, Homily 15, PL 76
.

msItem.title: Minus speculum peccatoris
msItem.incipit: Vacate et videte quoniam ego sum deus (Ps. 45:11) ysaye xlvi Ineffabilis diuine bonitatis dignacio que cottidie conspicit nos infelices
msItem.explicit: ut obitum beatum obtines et pervenias ad locum Inmortalitatis et felicitatis ac Iocunditatis eterne et cetera
msItem.finalRubric: Explicit mynus speculum peccatoris et cetera
msItem.note:
In the upper margin is added in black ink by a contemporary hand: Minus speculum peccatoris ex dictis sancti bernhardi. Listed in Bloomfield
. This text is usually ascribed to Augustine but here, as in the manuscript Stockholm, Kungliga biblioteket, MS A 198, f. 157r-158v, to Bernard of Clairvaux. Between the end of the text proper and the final rubric is a short text marked in the outer margin with nota bene and beginning Flos monachorum ad cor redeas Vide quid agas and ending Mentem compone perfeccioni stude deum time ut iudicem dilige ut patrem.

msItem.title: Formula honestae vitae with added excerpts
msItem.rubric: Formula beati bernhardi de vita et honestate
msItem.incipit: Petis a me frater karissime quod numquam et nusquam a suo provisore audiui petiuisse
msItem.explicit: lis in capitulo dissolucio in chor Irreuerencia circa altare hoc hugo de claustro anime et cetera et sic est finis huius
msItem.note:
The text of Formula honestae vitae as printed in PL 184
, ends on where nota bene has been added in both the inner and the outer margin in black and red ink respectively. Thereafter, follow short excerpts on themes such as the perfection of religious life, contempt for the world, and the value of correction, each introduced by a rubric in red or by the words nota bene. The last excerpt is from Ch. 11 of De claustro animae by Hugh of Fouilloy. On the original final rubric in red, Explicit tractatus vtilis videlicet formula beati bernhardi de vita et honestate, has been struck through with black ink. On is added in black ink by a contemporary hand: explicit formula bernhardi.

msItem.title: Treatise on the Eucharist
msItem.rubric: Incipit tractatus bonus de sacramento eukaristie
msItem.incipit: Mare non habundat tot guttis sol splendoribus nec silue frondibus
msItem.explicit: tentus non dimittatur donec in amoris cubiculum mansurus introducatur et cetera
msItem.finalRubric: Explicit tractatus bonus de sacramento eukaristie et cetera
msItem.note: This treatise seems to consist of two excerpts. The first one ending on and the second one beginning on where Item de sacramento is written in red in the upper margin.

watermark.note: Only parts of the watermark is visible in the upper margin.
The watermark is in the gutter and only partially visible.

supportDesc.foliation: Contemporary foliation in upper margin in ink 1-91, 95-149. The original ff. 92-94 and one unfoliated leaf have been ripped out.
supportDesc.condition: Four leaves ripped out between and . An unknown number of quires are missing after .

layout.note: Ruling in ink. Prickings in the margins.
Ruling in ink. Prickings in the margins.
Guidelines in ink. Text written above the top line and sometimes also below.

handDesc.handNote: Cursiva antiquior. Possibly a new hand on .
Cursiva antiquior with occasional loopless ascenders.
Cursiva antiquior. Possibly a new hand on and on .
Cursiva antiquior. Possibly a new hand on .

p.note: Upper margin in black ink by a later hand: Cartusie Erfordiensis; ca eight words by a contemporary hand but struck-through and only partially readable due to wear of the paper, the last words being scilicet in ultimo folio. In bottom margin in black ink, struck-through, by a contemporary hand: Sermones de tempore hora est iam nos et in fine priuilegia sanctorum; in light brown ink two illegible words have been added by a contemporary hand; in bottom right corner in black ink by a 19th-century hand: Inköpt till Kgl. Bibl. från G. Stephens 23/10 1848.
Nota-signs, manicules and short additions throughout, e.g. , , , , . On a longer addition in the outer margin which seems to have once been covered by a glued-on paper (?) slip, now lost.

decoDesc.p: Main text in black ink, rubrics in red or underlined or struck-through in red. Capitals touched in red. Plain initials over two lines in red.

decoNote.p: Bookmarks on (red parchment), , , , , , , and (all in white parchment).
